senses.definition: A sensation or image in the mind that continues for a short time after the thing causing it has stopped.
examples: When she closed her eyes, the bright light left an afterimpression that slowly faded.
The loud music created an afterimpression in his ears long after the concert ended.

senses.definition: The last milk that comes out when a cow is being milked, often thought to be of poorer quality.
examples: The farmer poured the afterings into a separate bucket.
All that was left in the cow were the thin afterings at the end of milking.

senses.definition: The last small part of any substance, usually of worse quality than what came before.
examples: He drank the afterings of his coffee from the bottom of the cup.
By the time we arrived, only the afterings of the meal were left on the table.

senses.definition: A king or ruler who comes after another king, especially as a successor to the throne.
examples: The afterking tried to continue the policies of his famous father.
Legends say the afterking was weaker than the first great ruler.

senses.definition: Knowledge or understanding that you gain only after something has already happened.
examples: With afterknowledge, we can see the mistakes in our decision.
His criticism was based on afterknowledge, not on what they knew at the time.

senses.definition: A life that some people believe continues after death.
examples: Many religions teach that there is an afterlife.
She wondered what the afterlife might be like.

senses.definition: The later reputation, influence, or continued existence of something after its end.
examples: The book enjoyed a rich afterlife in academic debate.
Her ideas had a long afterlife in political movements.

senses.definition: Plural of afterlife, used especially when talking about different ideas or versions of life after death.
examples: Different religions imagine very different afterlifes.
The novel compares the afterlifes promised by various belief systems.

senses.definition: The time or existence that is imagined to come after a person’s normal lifetime.
examples: The story follows the hero into an afterlifetime beyond earthly death.
In this myth, choices made in life shape the afterlifetime.

senses.definition: The light that remains in the sky after the sun has gone down; twilight or lingering light.
examples: They walked home in the soft afterlight of the sunset.
The mountains were outlined against the pale afterlight.

senses.definition: The period, feelings, or situation that follow a serious loss, especially emotional or financial.
examples: In the afterloss of the disaster, many families struggled to rebuild.
The book describes the quiet afterloss that comes after grief.

senses.definition: The emotional state or relationship that exists after a love affair has ended or changed.
examples: The film explores the strange afterlove between the former couple.
In the quiet afterlove, they learned to be friends again.

senses.definition: A mark or trace left on something after an event or after something has acted on it.
examples: The aftermark of the wound stayed on his skin for years.
Only the faint aftermark of the sticker remained on the glass.

senses.definition: The market for parts, accessories, or equipment that are made and sold by companies other than the original maker, especially for cars or machines.
examples: He bought an exhaust system from the aftermarket.
The company focuses on the automotive aftermarket.

senses.definition: Made or sold for the aftermarket, not by the original manufacturer.
examples: He installed aftermarket wheels on his car.
Aftermarket products can be cheaper than original parts.

senses.definition: Plural of aftermarket, referring to different markets for replacement parts or accessories.
examples: They operate in several regional aftermarkets.
Digital goods have created new aftermarkets for software tools.

senses.definition: The period or situation that comes after marriage, especially focusing on how the relationship changes.
examples: The novel looks at the quiet aftermarriage of the couple.
They struggled with the expectations of aftermarriage life.

senses.definition: The time or activities that happen after a church mass, especially social gatherings.
examples: They stayed for the aftermass to talk with friends.
Tea was served at the parish hall during the aftermass.

senses.definition: On a ship with several masts, the mast that is closest to the back (stern) of the ship.
examples: The flag was raised on the aftermast.
Damage to the aftermast made the ship hard to control.

senses.definition: The situation or effects that remain after a bad event, such as a war, disaster, or accident.
examples: Many people lost their homes in the aftermath of the storm.
The country is still dealing with the aftermath of the conflict.

senses.definition: Plural of aftermath, referring to several sets of consequences following events.
examples: The wars left deep and lasting aftermaths across the region.
They studied the social and economic aftermaths of the crisis.

senses.definition: Material that comes after the main part of a book or document, such as an index or notes.
examples: The glossary appears in the aftermatter of the textbook.
She checked the aftermatter for additional references.

senses.definition: Happening or used after a meal.
examples: He enjoys an aftermeal walk every evening.
The doctor recommended an aftermeal tablet to help digestion.

senses.definition: The time that comes just after a meal.
examples: In the quiet aftermeal, they sat and talked.
He used the aftermeal to read the newspaper.

senses.definition: Milk that comes from a mother’s breast after birth, especially connected with feeding or soothing a baby.
examples: The app plays sounds that remind babies of the aftermilk period.
Parents sought ways to recreate the gentle noises of aftermilk feeding time.

senses.definition: Furthest towards the back (stern) of a ship or vehicle.
examples: The aftermost cabin was small but quiet.
They stored the equipment in the aftermost part of the hold.

senses.definition: The time after night has passed; the early morning following the night.
examples: In the cold afternight, the city slowly woke up.
They arrived in the grey afternight before sunrise.

senses.definition: The part of the day from about 12:00 until the evening.
examples: I have a meeting this afternoon.
They played football in the afternoon.

senses.definition: Plural of afternoon, often used to talk about a regular time in the day on many days.
examples: She works in the shop on Monday and Tuesday afternoons.
In summer, we often swim in the lake on hot afternoons.

senses.definition: The back or rear part of the nose; used rarely, sometimes in anatomical or humorous contexts.
examples: The doctor joked that the injury was on the afternose, not the tip.
A slight shadow lay along the afternose in the photograph.

senses.definition: A taste, smell, or feeling that you notice at the end of an experience, especially in food or drink.
examples: The wine had a bitter afternote.
There was a smoky afternote in the coffee.

senses.definition: A short comment or note added at the end of a piece of writing or speech.
examples: The author included an afternote to explain the sources.
In an afternote, she thanked her colleagues.

senses.definition: An oar placed near the back of a boat, used for steering or rowing.
examples: The helmsman gripped the afteroar tightly.
They added an afteroar to improve control in rough water.

senses.definition: Pain that continues or returns after the main cause or event, especially the pain felt after childbirth as the womb returns to normal.
examples: She experienced strong afterpain following the birth.
Medication can help with the afterpain after surgery.

senses.definition: Pains felt after childbirth as the womb contracts and returns to its usual size.
examples: The nurse explained that her afterpains would lessen in a few days.
Afterpains can be stronger with each new baby.

senses.definition: The rear part of something, especially the back part of a ship or vehicle.
examples: The cargo was stored in the afterpart of the ship.
Passengers were seated mostly in the afterpart of the plane.

senses.definition: Parties that happen after a main event, such as a concert, show, or ceremony.
examples: Celebrities were invited to several afterparties after the awards show.
The band played a secret set at one of the afterparties.

senses.definition: A party that takes place after a main event, often with a smaller or more select group.
examples: We were invited to the afterparty after the premiere.
The afterparty went on until early morning.

senses.definition: Coming after something that is already in the past; belonging to a later past time.
examples: The novel shifts between early memories and afterpast reflections.
In his afterpast writings, he judged his youth harshly.

senses.definition: The part of a ship’s hold nearest the stern, often used as a storage space.
examples: The spare anchors were stored in the afterpeak.
Water collected in the afterpeak during the storm.

senses.definition: The period or level that follows the highest point of something such as demand, activity, or performance.
examples: Ticket prices dropped in the afterpeak of the season.
During the afterpeak, the service industry slowed down.

senses.definition: Plural of afterpeak, referring either to several rear compartments of ships or several post-peak periods.
examples: Engineers inspected the afterpeaks of both vessels.
Different markets went through their afterpeaks at different times.

senses.definition: A short play, musical piece, or performance that is given after the main play or show.
examples: The theatre presented a light afterpiece following the tragedy.
The afterpiece was a comic sketch that made the audience laugh.

senses.definition: Something that comes after the main part of a work, such as a short text or section.
examples: An interpretive essay served as an afterpiece to the novel.
The director added a brief afterpiece to explain the film’s ending.

senses.definition: Plural of afterpiece, referring to several short works or performances presented after main ones.
examples: The festival was known for its inventive afterpieces.
Old theatres often paired serious dramas with comic afterpieces.

senses.definition: Care, growth, or activities that take place after seeds or young plants have been put in the ground.
examples: Good afterplanting care is essential for a healthy crop.
The manual includes a section on afterplanting irrigation.

senses.definition: Activity or interaction that happens after the main event of play, performance, or intense experience, sometimes used for intimate situations.
examples: The workshop highlighted the importance of emotional afterplay after intense scenes.
They enjoyed quiet afterplay, talking and laughing together.

senses.definition: A small electrical change in a nerve or muscle cell that happens after the main action potential.
examples: The graph shows the afterpotential following the spike.
Researchers measured the afterpotential in nerve cells under different conditions.

senses.definition: Pressure that continues or appears after the main pressure event, often in mechanical or physical systems.
examples: Engineers monitored the afterpressure in the system.
The valve is designed to release excess afterpressure.

senses.definition: Evidence or proof that appears after an event, often confirming or changing earlier beliefs.
examples: The afterproof showed that his theory was correct.
We reassessed the case in light of the afterproof.

senses.definition: To rake or clean again after an initial raking, especially soil, gravel, or ashes.
examples: He had to afterrake the gravel to make it even.
Workers will afterrake the pitch before the next game.

senses.definition: A judgment or calculation made after an event, often about responsibility, cost, or results.
examples: In the afterreckoning, they realized the project had cost far too much.
The political afterreckoning lasted for years.

senses.definition: A support, beam, or structural piece set behind another, especially in a ship or carriage, to give extra strength.
examples: The shipbuilder added an afterrider to reinforce the stern.
A damaged afterrider can weaken the whole frame.

senses.definition: The process by which seeds or fruit continue to ripen after they are picked or after they reach a certain stage.
examples: Afterripening is necessary for some seeds to germinate.
The fruit undergoes afterripening during storage.

senses.definition: Movement or rolling that continues after the main roll has stopped, especially in a ship or aircraft.
examples: The vessel’s afterroll made walking difficult.
Engineers studied the afterroll to improve stability.

senses.definition: A sweet dish eaten after the main part of a meal; dessert (mainly British English, informal).
examples: What would you like for afters?
We had ice cream for afters.
